Title: Regulation of metabolic gene clusters in Arabidopsis thaliana

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes an oxidative desaturation/elaboration step in thalianol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: thalianol pathway intermediate leading to thalianol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for pathway elaboration toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in roots, low in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: specialized metabolism implicated in development and ecological interactions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced THAH transcript in arp6 and hta9/hta11 backgrounds as part of coordinated repression of the thalianol cluster, associated with reduced thalianol accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in chromatin mutants; GC-MS metabolite profiling; ChIP for H2A.Z; nucleosome positioning
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seedling roots versus leaves; chromatin mutant backgrounds arp6 and hta9/hta11
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Positive expression association with ARP6-dependent H2A.Z deposition across the cluster; higher H2A.Z enrichment in roots; increased nucleosome occupancy in arp6 mutant correlates with reduced expression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THAH was used as the representative thalianol cluster gene in the chromatin mutant screen. 2. THAH transcript levels were strongly reduced in arp6 and also reduced in hta9/hta11. 3. THAH belongs to a root-expressed metabolic gene cluster whose coordinated expression depends on ARP6/H2A.Z-mediated chromatin remodeling.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper focuses on cluster-level chromatin regulation rather than biochemical dissection of THAH catalysis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating chromatin regulators such as ARP6/H2A.Z could enable coordinated tuning of whole specialized-metabolite clusters rather than single enzyme genes
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential pleiotropic effects expected from altering global chromatin regulators are not quantified in this paper

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: chromatin remodeling factor; SWR1 complex component
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Actin-related protein 6; SWR1 chromatin-remodeling complex component
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Promotes H2A.Z deposition into nucleosomes, leading to chromatin opening and transcriptional activation of clustered pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis of clustered specialized triterpenes through coordinate cluster expression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: THAS; THAD; THAH; ACT; MRN1; CYP705A12; MRO
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates/coordinately promotes expression of thalianol and marneral cluster genes while flanking genes remain largely unaffected
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive chromatin regulator required for normal expression of two Arabidopsis metabolic gene clusters and for normal thalianol accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions as part of the SWR1 chromatin remodeling complex
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Function inferred in roots where clusters are expressed
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: specialized metabolites implicated in development and ecological interactions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: arp6 mutation reduces transcript levels of thalianol and marneral cluster genes and significantly lowers thalianol accumulation in roots
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis in Col-0 and Ler; qRT-PCR; GC-MS metabolite profiling; ChIP; nucleosome positioning
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: root versus leaf tissue; arp6 mutant background
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Required for deposition of histone variant H2A.Z across thalianol and marneral clusters; arp6 mutants show increased nucleosome occupancy/stability and reduced cluster expression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ARP6 is required for normal transcript accumulation of genes in both thalianol and marneral clusters. 2. arp6 roots show reduced thalianol levels without altering sitosterol, indicating specific impact on specialized metabolism. 3. ARP6 promotes a more open local chromatin state through H2A.Z deposition across cluster regions.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Effects are localized to clustered genes relative to immediately flanking genes; cluster expression is reduced but not abolished, implying additional regulators exist.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Represents a lever for coordinated activation of entire metabolic gene clusters in metabolic engineering, though specificity issues must be considered
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely broad pleiotropic effects from manipulating a chromatin remodeling factor; not quantified here

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive chromatin-based regulation associated with active cluster expression; deposition across cluster reduces nucleosome stability and facilitates transcription
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: coordinate expression of triterpene biosynthetic gene clusters
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: THAS; THAD; THAH; ACT; MRN1; CYP705A12; MRO
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes expression of thalianol and marneral cluster genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: H2A.Z enrichment across clusters positively correlates with active expression, and hta9/hta11 mutation reduces cluster transcripts
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Deposited by the SWR1 complex involving ARP6
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher H2A.Z enrichment at clusters in roots than leaves
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: specialized metabolites implicated in development and ecological interactions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of HTA9/HTA11 reduces transcript levels of all thalianol and marneral cluster genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: hta9/hta11 double mutant analysis; qRT-PCR; H2A.Z-GFP ChIP in roots and leaves
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native promoters; HTA11:GFP transgene used for ChIP
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: root versus leaf tissue; hta9/hta11 mutant background
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: H2A.Z enrichment spans whole cluster regions and is higher in roots, positively correlating with expression; reduced deposition in arp6 is associated with higher nucleosome occupancy
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. H2A.Z is enriched across the thalianol cluster and also implicated at the marneral cluster. 2. H2A.Z abundance is higher in roots where clusters are active than in leaves where they are inactive. 3. hta9/hta11 mutants phenocopy arp6 with reduced cluster gene expression, supporting a direct positive chromatin role.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study does not claim complete exclusivity of H2A.Z control; additional regulators likely contribute.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suggests epigenome engineering of H2A.Z deposition could coordinately tune specialized-metabolite clusters
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation of histone variants may cause broad genome-wide effects
